Details: Chlorine was found to be ineffective in the removal of six of the seven alachlor degradates evaluated in surface water according to the results of one bench-scale study. Generally, removal ranged from 0 to 16 percent with a dose of 6 mg/L of chlorine and 6 hours of contact time. Greater than 99 percent removal, however, was achieved for 2,6-diethylaniline under the same conditions.
